\documentclass[english]{article}
\usepackage[T1]{fontenc}
\usepackage[latin9]{luainputenc}
\usepackage{babel}
\begin{document}
\title{Dread}

\maketitle
Sometimes at night, a soothing figure of the one I loved is given
birth from the lingering silence, formed by the freedom that I was
going to taste, I imagined that it was her offering it to me. I wanted
to give everything to it, my own heat, my body. Suddenly, I awaken.
And the faint memory of the girl I met before dreaming fades away. 

When you wake up, your spirit searches vainly for where you are, where
were you when you fell asleep. The furniture wakes up with you, it's
immobility is imposed by you, and it's memory is formed by a sleeper
who felt too alone at night admist the slumbering chairs, doors and
tables. You try to pinpoint where your limbs are after a swirling
night of darkess, trying to guess it's orientation, it's existence.
Mornings cannot be less dreadful.

I take sleeping pills at night, wake up before my sleep is restful
enough, only to find myself unable to fall back asleep, and I drink
coffee to stay awake during the day.
\end{document}
